pages.raw_text:
The news from Olympia that the
state supreme court has reversed the
decision Of Judge Linn of the Thurs
ton county court denying relief to the
teachers of the state normal school
at Cheney. who sued for their salaries
on account of a breach of contract
by the local board of trustees. is re-
ceived with much interest in Spokane,
where all the parties are we'll known
Mrs. Rose Rice Turner, who receives
a verdict of $1,000 and costs, was the
democratic nominee for Spokane coun-
ty school superintendent last year.
Although she ran over 200 votes
ahead of her ticket. she was yet I'M
able to overcome the republican ma
jority. Miss E. May Mackenzie, who
gets verdict of $500 and costs, is
one of the best known kindergarten
teachers in this city and is a daugh-
ter of Mr.. and Mrs. Roderick Mac
Kenzie, owners of the Liberty Lake
hotel.


The local board of trustees. who
were co-defendants with the state,
consisted of J. ]. Browne, Joseph s.
Allen and E. Dempsie. Later N. w.
Durham Of the Spokesman Review
succeeded Mr.. Dempsie, and became
an active party to the resistance of
the claims of the teachers and edi-
torially commended Judge Linn's de-
cision against the teachers in the
lower court. Graves & Graves con-
Quoted the case for the plaintiffs, the
history of which Is in brief as fol-
lows:


In the spring of 1900 Principal w.
B. Turner Of the state normal school
at Cheney resigned and accepted po-
sition with the Equitable Life Assur-
ance society in this city, which posi-
ton he still holds. The board re-
elected the old faculty, including his
wife. Mrs. Turner, principal of the
